refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety, clarifying the performance requirement for teleoperation communication.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of the teleoperation communication link under defined network conditions using telemetry logs and network monitoring tools.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission under agreed network conditions.
FR3: Provide timely feedback to the operator to maintain safe control.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency (e.g., UDP with minimal retransmissions).
DP2: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees.
DP3: Implement edge processing to reduce data volume and processing delay.
DP4: Prioritize teleoperation data packets in network scheduling.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency (e.g., UDP with minimal retransmissions).

potential_sources: Dependency on network link quality and scheduling.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on protocol optimization, which is logically separable from physical network infrastructure and safety services, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline performance.
Risk that protocol optimization alone may not guarantee latency under all conditions.
Risk of packet loss impacting control responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP4 in future iterations to address network-level latency guarantees and prioritization.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication stack responsible for teleoperation data handling and protocol implementation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network interface used for teleoperation data transmission.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending telemetry data to monitoring and logging systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands for teleoperation control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains multiple functionalities beyond teleoperation, leading to higher diffusion.
Primarily physical components; teleoperation protocol logic is not contained here.
Highest containment score; dedicated module for teleoperation logic with minimal diffusion.
Focuses on safety alerts and monitoring, not teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Teleoperation module is the best architectural boundary to contain the optimized communication protocol stack for latency management, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Tasking modules.
Risk of latency impact if Teleoperation module is not tightly integrated with physical network hardware.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions and integration testing plans to ensure latency targets are met across module boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR by focusing on protocol optimization to meet latency requirements.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as Teleoperation module centralizes protocol logic.
Interface complexity with Network Link requires careful management.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for the selected DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified through telemetry and network monitoring at the Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module for latency management.

recommended_revisions: Expand DP selection to consider network-level QoS in next iteration.
Define detailed interface contracts between Teleoperation and Network Link modules.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of DP to include network QoS and prioritization is recommended to fully address latency under all conditions.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission under agreed network conditions.
FR3: Provide timely feedback to the operator to maintain safe control.

revise.why: To incorporate additional design parameters addressing network-level guarantees and data prioritization for robust latency performance.

assumptions: Network conditions meet agreed baseline performance.
Teleoperation module can implement protocol optimizations effectively.
open_risks: Latency may not be guaranteed under all network conditions with current DP.
Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Network Link modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data from prototype implementations.
Network condition characterization and QoS capability assessment.
